task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Import the survey points CSV file into TopoCal
task_completion.visual_evidence: An import dialog showing 'colorado_site_survey.csv' being loaded, or the survey points are visible in the TopoCal workspace.

task_completion.description: Initiate the export to Google Earth (KML) process
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box or menu specifically for exporting to Google Earth or KML is open in TopoCal.
In the export dialog, the Hemisphere (Hemisferio) is set to North (Norte) and the UTM Zone (Huso) is set to 13.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting KML file to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog shows the file being saved as 'site_export.kml' in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\', or a file explorer window confirms the file's creation in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal GUI to perform the export
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with TopoCal menus and dialogs, rather than using a terminal or script to generate the KML.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create the KML file using a text editor
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no screenshots of Notepad, VS Code, or other text editors open with KML code.

integrity.description: The agent interacted genuinely with the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog matches the UI style of TopoCal and the agent is seen clicking through the actual application interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is C:/Users/Docker/Documents/site_export.kml. The export must be configured for UTM Zone 13 and Hemisphere Norte (North). The resulting KML should contain at least 50 points and fall within the bounding box: min_lon -106.0, max_lon -104.0, min_lat 39.0, max_lat 41.0.
